title: Space Force eyes 2027 demonstrations of in-space refueling and satellite servicing
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to conduct in-space refueling and satellite servicing demonstrations by 2027. The USSF-23 mission will launch vehicles to test these capabilities in geostationary orbit.

title: SpaceX files for IPO
summary: SpaceX has filed documents for an initial public offering (IPO) on May 20, revealing its financial details and ambitious plans. This move marks a significant step for the company as it seeks to go public.

title: U.S. ‘more prepared’ for next WRC
summary: The White House states the U.S. will be better prepared for the next World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC) to defend its radio-frequency spectrum priorities. The announcement highlights improved readiness ahead of the major international conference next year.

title: Orbital Data Centers: Power and Thermal Management for Scalable Architectures
summary: A new whitepaper by Redwire explores the challenges of power generation and thermal management for scalable orbital data centers as they transition from concept to reality. Efficient solutions in these areas are highlighted as critical enabling technologies.

title: Starfighters turns Texas facility toward microgravity flight testing
summary: Starfighters Space is converting its Texas facility to support microgravity flight testing in response to NASA's request for commercial parabolic flight capabilities. The company, which develops F-104 supersonic jets for satellite air-launch, aims to leverage its aircraft for research and testing in microgravity environments.

title: Report finds U.S. space supply chains rely heavily on Chinese manufacturing
summary: A report by Altana reveals that U.S. space supply chains are heavily dependent on Chinese manufacturing, with additional reliance on Taiwanese semiconductor production and upstream exposure to Chinese and Russian components.

title: The cardinality wall: The hidden data bottleneck for LEO constellations
summary: The satellite industry is facing a data bottleneck due to the massive scale of LEO constellations, which are growing rapidly to support broadband and national security applications.

title: Isaacman expects Chinese crewed mission around the moon in 2027
summary: NASA's head expects China to conduct a crewed lunar flyby mission in 2027, intensifying the perceived space race between China and the U.S.

title: SDA director Sandhoo takes on broader Space Force missile warning portfolio
summary: The Pentagon is realigning the Space Development Agency’s satellite programs under a new acquisition structure, with SDA director Sandhoo taking on a broader role in the Space Force's missile warning portfolio.

title: Startup challenging satellite industry’s multi-orbit playbook
summary: A startup named Contrivian is challenging the satellite industry's traditional multi-orbit approach, arguing that LEO constellations alone can offer better resilience and speed. The company claims that combining GEO, MEO, and LEO satellites creates networking problems.

title: Space debris is forcing satellites to dodge more often — costing us vital science. 'Things will get worse before they get better'
summary: Space debris is increasingly forcing satellites to perform avoidance maneuvers, leading to data loss and fuel consumption. Experts warn that the situation will deteriorate before improving.

title: Scientists find a hidden route to the moon that saves fuel
summary: An international team of researchers discovered a more fuel-efficient route to the moon using advanced computer modeling. This breakthrough could significantly reduce the cost and complexity of lunar missions.

title: SpaceX stacks Starship V3 rocket, completes major fueling test ahead of debut launch
summary: SpaceX has completed stacking the Starship V3 rocket and Super Heavy booster at Starbase, preparing for the vehicle's 12th test flight. The company also conducted a major fueling test ahead of the debut launch.

title: US military test-launches nuclear-capable ICBM from California (photos)
summary: The U.S. military conducted a test launch of an unarmed intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on May 20, 2026. The test was part of routine missile defense and deterrence efforts.

title: Where did Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid come from? It may be the only survivor of the planet's violent history
summary: Neptune's moon Nereid may be the only surviving satellite from the planet's original system, according to a new study. Researchers suggest it holds clues to Neptune's violent past.

title: NASA satellite images uncover dramatic changes in Earth's nighttime lights
summary: NASA's Black Marble project uses satellite images to show dramatic changes in Earth's nighttime lights over nearly a decade, revealing shifts in human activity visible from space.

title: The moon shines with a swarm of stars on May 21 as Jupiter, Venus and Mercury line-up nearby
summary: On May 21, the moon appeared near the Beehive Cluster while Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury aligned in the evening sky. This celestial event provided a striking visual display for stargazers.

title: An astronaut's view of Argentina's snow-capped mountains | Space photo of the day for May 20, 2026
summary: An astronaut captured a stunning space photo of Argentina's snow-capped mountains. The image highlights the beauty of Earth from space.

title: NASA's Psyche probe takes awesome images of Mars on way to (possibly) precious asteroid
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft performed a gravity assist flyby of Mars, capturing close-up images during its journey to the asteroid Psyche. The mission aims to explore the potentially valuable asteroid.

title: Starship Flight 12: Welcome to the Show Block 3 and Pad 2
summary: Starship Flight 12 marks the debut of Block 3 and Pad 2, seven years after the first Raptor engine test. The launch signifies a major milestone in SpaceX's Starship development program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key advancement in SpaceX's launch capabilities.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is progressing with planning and processing for Artemis III, a significant crewed mission. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) testing.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. This marks a significant expansion for the company in the space industry.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a new launch pad for its Starship rocket, which is now preparing for its first launch. The redesigned pad marks a significant upgrade in SpaceX's launch infrastructure.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt, following a previous docking by Progress MS-34. The mission marks another resupply effort to the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are scheduled for nine launches in the week of May 11. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic, but the specific details of the news were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young minds, test and validate materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for the agency's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests testing or preparation of parachutes for space missions. The specific details about the event or technology are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved docking and debris capture. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and space debris mitigation efforts.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on 8-9 June 2026.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO. The company is expected to go public next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race rather than protect the U.S. The system is intended to serve as a defensive measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve collaborations with Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over scientific partnerships with China.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews. This shift aims to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000 depending on the scenario, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, and high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial, marking a significant move in the space industry. This acquisition highlights York Space Systems' active merger and acquisition activities.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission. The company is set to launch a rover to the Moon later this year.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and manage lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional mining and infrastructure on the Moon.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has selected Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's service is gaining significant traction in the space industry.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first public glimpse into its financials. This move marks a significant step for the company as it plans to go public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily feature satellite-related discussions, impacting the global space sector.

title: Redwire Taps Voyager for Subcontract for DARPA Otter Mission
summary: Redwire has awarded Voyager Technologies a subcontract for the DARPA Otter mission to supply its high-precision Acceleration Measurement System (AMS). The mission aims to advance space technology for defense research.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs Want Multiple Constellations for D2D Service
summary: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs discussed their satellite strategies for direct-to-device (D2D) services, emphasizing the need for multiple satellite constellations. The CEOs made these remarks at an investor event following their joint venture announcement.

title: Iceye Launches New Solution for Banking Sector
summary: Iceye has launched a new natural catastrophe monitoring solution tailored for the international banking sector. The offering aims to provide risk assessment and mitigation services for financial institutions.

title: HackerOne Exec Explains Key Security Trends in the Age of AI
summary: Generative AI is increasingly being used as a weapon by hackers, posing significant cybersecurity challenges. HackerOne, a key player in cybersecurity, is addressing these emerging threats.

title: Securing the Return to the Moon
summary: The article discusses the importance of securing the return to the Moon by learning from past events and challenges. It emphasizes the need for strategic planning and international cooperation to ensure sustainable lunar exploration.

title: Sandhoo Named SDA Director, PAE for Missile Warning and Tracking
summary: Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo was officially named director of the Space Development Agency (SDA) on May 11, following his acting role since September. The SDA also announced a potential acquisition (PAE) for missile warning and tracking systems.

title: Northrop Grumman’s First MRV Readies for Summer Launch to Expand the Space Servicing Toolkit
summary: Northrop Grumman’s SpaceLogistics is preparing to launch its first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) this summer for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The MRV aims to expand the company's space servicing capabilities.
